channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: U.S. Is Negotiating an Iran Deal That Would Buy Time, Again
item.description: The United States proposed a 20-year “suspension” of all nuclear activity, even as President Trump demands assurances that Iran can never build a nuclear weapon.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance arriving at Joint Base Andrews from Islamabad, Pakistan, on Sunday. Mr. Vance there were “some good conversations” with Iran in Pakistan, and the ball is now in Tehran’s court.

item.title: Amid War, G.O.P. Puts Off Testimony From Senior Pentagon Commanders
item.description: House lawmakers had been scheduled to hear testimony next week from the head of military operations in the Middle East, but the Republican majority postponed the hearing until late May.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: “We are six weeks into this conflict, and we still haven’t gotten a public briefing from anyone in the administration about the war,” said Representative Adam Smith of Washington, the ranking Democrat on the Armed Services Committee.

item.title: What the U.S. Blockade of Iran and the Strait of Hormuz Might Look Like
item.description: The U.S. military has provided few details on how it might carry out President Trump’s orders as he seeks to pressure Tehran on a peace deal. But history and established practices offer some clues.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A ship in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Oman on Sunday.

item.title: U.S. Begins a Blockade of the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: European countries declined to take part in the action, which is designed to pressure Iran into making concessions by cutting off its oil income.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Peter Eavis, Jenny Gross and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A cargo ship off the coast of Ras al-Khaimah, United Arab Emirates, on Monday.

item.title: As Internet Blackout Hits Day 45, Iranians Bemoan State Hypocrisy and Economic Risks
item.description: Iranians enduring the latest communications shutdown warn that it’s crippling businesses. Critics say officials flagrantly use the internet they block citizens from.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: An internet blackout in Iran has shut down communications for most of its more than 90 million citizens.

item.title: Two Iran-linked Ships Passed Through Strait of Hormuz Ahead of U.S. Blockade
item.description: The vessels exited the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, hours before a U.S. naval blockade took effect.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz last week. Traffic in the waterway has slowed since the start of the conflict.

item.title: Iran Blockade Sets Up a Test of Which Side Can Endure More Pain
item.description: President Trump is trying to choke off the country’s lifeline with a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. But the Iranians are betting that his tolerance for political pain is limited.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Fuel prices reflected on a semi-truck in Aurora, Ore., last week. “Soon you’ll be nostalgic for $4 to $5 gas,” Iran’s top negotiator warned American consumers after peace talks fell apart over the weekend.

item.title: UK Will Not Join U.S. Blockade of Strait of Hormuz, Starmer Says
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer said the U.K.’s diplomatic efforts are directed at keeping the vital waterway “open, not shut.”
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain, who visited British troops in the Middle East last week, said Monday he is focused on “bringing countries together to keep the straits open, not shut.”

item.title: To Open the Strait of Hormuz, Trump Wants to Blockade Iran. Experts Are Skeptical.
item.description: President Trump’s announcement of a U.S. blockade was his latest effort to pressure Iran to negotiate after direct talks over the weekend did not yield an agreement.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A new billboard in Tehran on Monday with text that translates to “Forever in Iran’s hands” and “Trump couldn’t do a damn thing. Iran will have the control over the Strait of Hormuz forever.”

item.title: Israelis Don’t Feel Much Like Victors in War With Iran
item.description: The regime in Iran has not changed and the nuclear and missile threats have not been eliminated, leaving many Israelis to wonder what this was all for.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
item.media:description: Activists held posters of war victims from around the region during a protest in Tel Aviv on Saturday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Eric Schmitt, Farnaz Fassihi and David E. Sanger

item.title: How Would a Blockade of the Strait of Hormuz Work? Here Are Some Possibilities.
item.description: The goal of the naval operation ordered by President Trump is to deny Iran the revenues it needs to fund its military, but there are many unknowns.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump said the U.S. would blockade the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Iran’s Underground Air Force Base Was Hit Earlier in the War
item.description: Satellite imagery shows impact craters at and near the tunnel entrances to aircraft shelters at Iran’s remote “Eagle 44” base.
item.dc:creator: Christoph Koettl
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Iran’s Top Negotiator Says Talks Failed Because U.S. Failed to Win Trust
item.description: Iranian officials said that while Iran had approached the negotiations in good faith, the U.S. team had not gained the Iranian delegation’s trust.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran on Saturday. Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, Iran’s top negotiator and the speaker of its Parliament, said the U.S. team had failed to “gain the trust of the Iranian delegation.”

item.title: Clearing naval mines is painstaking, dangerous work.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: How JD Vance Tried and Failed to End the War in Iran That He Opposed
item.description: Vice President JD Vance led the highest-level talks between the United States and Iran in nearly 50 years.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vice President Vance arriving for a news conference after meeting with representatives from Pakistan and Iran in Islamabad on Sunday.

item.title: Israel Launches New Attacks in Lebanon, Days Ahead of Rare Direct Talks
item.description: Israel’s campaign targeting Hezbollah in Lebanon has been a source of tension in the U.S.-Iran cease-fire. Israeli and Lebanese officials plan to meet for rare talks in Washington this week.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A building on Saturday in the Lebanese capital, Beirut, that was destroyed by Israeli strikes.

item.title: In Pakistan Talks, Iran Saw a U.S. Trying to Dictate, Not Negotiate
item.description: Iran sees American demands as reaching far beyond what the United States achieved in war. Tehran is gambling that it can withstand further bombardment more than Washington is willing to sustain economic chaos, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard of Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran’s supreme leader, on Saturday in Tehran. Iranian officials argue that the United States did not actually want to negotiate during talks this weekend in Pakistan.

item.title: Netanyahu Says War With Iran Is ‘Not Yet Over’
item.description: In a televised address, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel sought to placate critics at home who say the war failed to achieve its goals.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel in Jerusalem last month.

item.title: Inside Tyre, a Lebanese City Bombarded by Israel
item.description: In Tyre, a city on Lebanon’s coast, near-daily bombardments by Israel have killed and injured civilians, and left many searching for shelter.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum, Hwaida Saad and David Guttenfelder
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Lebanese medics transport a man critically wounded by an Israeli strike in the back of an ambulance outisde a hospital in Lebanon’s southern port city of Tyre, last month.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers, Tyler Pager, Aaron Boxerman and Isabel Kershner

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz and Iran’s Uranium Stockpiles Were Sticking Points in U.S.-Iran Peace Talks
item.description: The U.S. had demanded that Iran immediately reopen the strait to all maritime traffic, but Iran said it would do so only after a final peace deal, according to Iranian officials.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Journalists broadcasting on Saturday from the Jinnah Convention Center in Islamabad, Pakistan, near where Iranian and U.S. officials were meeting.

item.title: What Now? Vance Leaves Iran Talks Without a Deal.
item.description: The lack of a breakthrough after 21 hours of negotiations leaves the Trump administration facing several unpalatable options.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager and David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance leaving after negotiations with Iran in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Sunday.

item.title: U.S. Moves to Deport Son of Prominent Figure in Iranian Revolution
item.description: Federal agents arrested a man whose mother served as a spokeswoman for the Islamist embassy captors during the hostage crisis that began in 1979.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Trump administration announced plans to deport the son of Masoumeh Ebtekar, the former vice president of Iran, seen here during a news conference in 2019.

item.title: U.S.-Iran Talks Timeline: Key Moments Between Two Adversaries
item.description: From sanctions relief to nuclear talks, here’s a look at how negotiations between Washington and Tehran have gone over the decades.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Islamabad, the capital of Pakistan, on Friday as the city prepared to host the talks between the United States and Iran.

item.title: U.S. Intelligence Shows China Taking a More Active Role in Iran War
item.description: China may have shipped missiles to Iran, and Beijing is allowing some companies to sell Tehran supplies that can be used in military production, American officials said.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Eric Schmitt and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: An anti-U.S. billboard on Sunday in Tehran showing American aircraft captured in a net.

item.title: There are few public details about the high-level talks — not even the timing.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance being greeted on Saturday by Pakistani officials in Islamabad, Pakistan.

item.title: Many Iranians Are Glad the Fighting Has Paused. Some Hard-liners Aren’t.
item.description: The most fervent backers of the Islamic republic believe pausing the war for negotiations risks squandering what they see as a hard-won upper hand.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Iran Tries to Grasp Economic Devastation of War, and Find a Way Past It
item.description: The vast scale of destruction wrought by U.S. and Israeli bombardment will make sanctions relief all the more vital to Iran’s government as it tries to negotiate a peace agreement.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Assessing the aftermath of a U.S.-Israeli airstrike this month on a bridge in Karaj, Iran.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Farnaz Fassihi, Elian Peltier and Aaron Boxerman

item.title: Iran Looks to Project Unity With Large Delegation to Peace Talks
item.description: At least 70 people are in the team that is scheduled to negotiate with the American side in Pakistan on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Pakistan's Ministry of Foreign Affairs, via Reuters
item.media:description: A photograph released by Pakistan’s government showing the Iranian delegation led by the speaker of Iran’s parliament, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, second from right; and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, second from left. They were welcomed by Pakistan’s army chief, Asim Munir, left; and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar, right, in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Friday.

item.title: Iran Has Been Consistent in War. Will It Be Consistent in Peace Talks?
item.description: While President Trump’s war aims have changed by the moment, Iran has stuck to firm demands. The question is whether it will compromise in peace negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Pro-government demonstrators burning American and Israeli flags on Wednesday in Tehran after a cease-fire was announced.

item.title: In New War With Israel, Hezbollah Defies Notion That It Was Crippled
item.description: The Iran-backed Lebanese militant group surprised many with the intensity of its attacks on Israel in the current conflict.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: An Israeli soldier at the site of a Hezbollah missile strike in Haniel, Israel, last month.

item.title: World Leaders Push to Save Iran Talks Amid Israel’s Attacks in Lebanon
item.description: President Trump’s cease-fire with Iran appears at risk as Vice President JD Vance heads to Pakistan for discussions with Iranian officials.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A Lebanese man stood on a pile of debris in the Corniche El Mazraa area of Beirut after the Israeli bombardment of the capital on Wednesday.

item.title: What the Cease-Fire Means for Iran
item.description: Emerging from weeks of U.S. and Israeli airstrikes, an emboldened Iran has 10 demands for talks during the tenuous cease-fire, according to Iranian state media. Our reporter Erika Solomon assesses Iran’s position.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Christina Thornell, David Seekamp and Joey Sendaydiego

item.title: New Evidence Further Implicates U.S. Missiles in Strikes That Killed 21 Civilians in Iran
item.description: Additional images and video build on an earlier analysis, which the Pentagon has disputed, showing Precision Strike Missiles, or PrSMs, hit a sports hall and residential areas in the Iranian city of Lamerd.
item.dc:creator: Christiaan Triebert, John Ismay, Alexander Cardia, Leanne Abraham and Azmat Khan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: With U.S.-Iran Peace Talks Set to Begin, Prospects for Long-Term Deal Uncertain
item.description: With U.S. and Iranian leaders heading to Pakistan for negotiations amid a two-week cease-fire, the two sides must resolve major differences on Lebanon, the Strait of Hormuz and frozen Iranian assets.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati, Adam Rasgon, Euan Ward and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Before his departure on Friday for talks in Pakistan, Vice President JD Vance said he believed the negotiations were “going to be positive,” but he also had a warning for Iran.

item.title: Facing Many Crises, Pakistan Tries to End a Big One — in Iran
item.description: A nation with a stalled economy, a terrorism problem and two hostile neighbors is set to host the first formal U.S.-Iranian talks since the war began.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Watching coverage of the Iran war at a barbershop in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Friday.

item.title: Why Opening the Strait of Hormuz Won’t Immediately Lower Gas Prices
item.description: Our energy reporter Rebecca F. Elliott explains why, even if the flow of energy is restored through the Persian Gulf, it will take months to carry out repairs across dozens of energy sites in the region.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott, Coleman Lowndes, Alexandra Ostasiewicz, Stephanie Swart, Nikolay Nikolov and Thomas Vollkommer

item.title: Only two ships passed through the Strait of Hormuz on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.media:description: A tanker moored in Muscat, Oman, last month. Hundreds of ships are waiting on either side of the Strait of Hormuz as Iran and the United States seek to negotiate an end to the war.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon Plan Talks in D.C. as War Threatens U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire Deal
item.description: Whether Israel’s conflict with Hezbollah, the Iran-backed militant group in Lebanon, is included in the Iran cease-fire is one of the major disputes rattling the truce.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Adam Rasgon, Natan Odenheimer and Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Residents lifting debris from a damaged car in Beirut, Lebanon, in a neighborhood damaged by Israeli airstrikes earlier this week.

item.title: Iran Demands Release of ‘Blocked Assets’ Before Peace Talks
item.description: In what appeared to be a new condition, Iran’s parliament speaker said that negotiations set for Saturday in Pakistan would not go ahead unless unspecified Iranian funds were released.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Leily Nikounazar
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, the speaker of Iran’s Parliament, has introduced conditions that could derail talks with U.S. negotiators set for this weekend.

item.title: ‘I’m Fed Up.’ Frustrated With Trump, Starmer Embraces Other Allies.
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain is strengthening ties in Europe and the Middle East as the once-special relationship with America sours.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain boarding an airplane, in Doha, Qatar, on Friday, after a three-day trip to the Gulf.

item.title: European Airports Warn of Jet Fuel Shortages if Strait of Hormuz Remains Shut
item.description: An association of airports told European Union officials that fuel shipments through the Strait of Hormuz had to restart within three weeks to avoid a “systemic” shortage.
item.dc:creator: Niraj Chokshi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Travelers at Frankfurt Airport in Germany on Friday.

item.title: Fuel Protests Cause Transport Chaos in Ireland as Iran War Spikes Prices
item.description: The Irish government said it had called in the army after protesters blocked highways, ports and an oil refinery, causing widespread disruption.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Tractors blocking O’Connell Street in Dublin, a main artery of the capital, during a protest on Wednesday.
